title: nasturtium palustre var. tanacetifolium
intro: Nasturtium palustre var. tanacetifolium, commonly known as Marsh Cress or Marsh Tansy Cress, is a plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Nasturtium palustre var. tanacetifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in salad greens and as a garnish, although its culinary and medicinal uses require further research and validation.
